operations.notes:
Fetches a list of NAT objects for a given revision
Note: NAT objects are only relevant for Check Point management servers and Juniper Netscreen devices.
The default value for get_total is false. For API calls that have pagination, set get_total to true on the first call. Use the value to returned in total to determine how many additional calls are required to retrieve all the desired results. To improve performance, omit or set get_total to false on any subsequent calls.

parameters.name: context
parameters.description: Global MSSP context

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Version ID

parameters.name: start
parameters.description: Starting page for query allowable value is numeric

parameters.name: count
parameters.description: Number of pages for query starting from starting page, allowable type is numeric

parameters.name: get_total
parameters.description: Set to true to get the total number of items that can be retrieved

operations.notes:
Fetches a list of NAT rules for a given revision.
input_interface and output_interface are not supported for Panorama running in Advanced management mode
nat_stage is supported for Netscreen and JunOS
Supported devices:
Cisco (ASA), Juniper (SRX, Junos, NetScreen), and Palo Alto (Panorama management devices only, FW devices not supported).

parameters.name: context
parameters.description: Global MSSP context

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Revision ID

parameters.name: input_interface
parameters.description: Input interface for NAT rules, "any" for all interfaces

parameters.name: output_interface
parameters.description: output interface for NAT rules, "any" for all interfaces

parameters.name: nat_stage
parameters.description: NAT stage for NAT rules allowable values: "pre_policy" , "post_policy" or "both"

parameters.name: nat_type
parameters.description: NAT stage for NAT rules allowable values: "vip" or "reverse_vip"

operations.notes:
Fetches a list of NAT objects defined on device given by device ID
Note: NAT objects are only relevant for Check Point management servers and Juniper Netscreen devices.
The default value for get_total is false. For API calls that have pagination, set get_total to true on the first call. Use the value to returned in total to determine how many additional calls are required to retrieve all the desired results. To improve performance, omit or set get_total to false on any subsequent calls.

parameters.name: context
parameters.description: Global MSSP context

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Device ID

parameters.name: start
parameters.description: Starting page for query allowable value is numeric

parameters.name: count
parameters.description: Starting page for query allowable value is numeric

parameters.name: get_total
parameters.description: Set to true to get the total number of items that can be retrieved

operations.notes:
Fetches list of NAT rules for device given by device ID

input_interface and output_interface are not supported for Panorama running in Advanced management mode
nat_stage is supported for Netscreen and JunOS
nat_type is supported for FortiManager
Note: For Check Point, NAT rules are available from the modules (not managements)
Note: For Palo Alto, filtering NAT rules by interface is only available for firewall devices

parameters.name: context
parameters.description: Global MSSP context

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Device ID

parameters.name: input_interface
parameters.description: Input interface for NAT rules, "any" for all interfaces

parameters.name: output_interface
parameters.description: output interface for NAT rules, "any" for all interfaces

parameters.name: nat_stage
parameters.description: NAT stage for NAT rules allowable values: "pre_policy" , "post_policy" or "both"

parameters.name: nat_type
parameters.description: NAT stage for NAT rules allowable values: "vip" or "reverse_vip"

protocol.description: The port number, for IP service: protocol is not specified.

min.description: For IP, TCP and UDP Services: the minimum port number, ICMP Service: the ICMP type number.

max.description: For IP, TCP and UDP Services: the maximum port number.

negate.description: Negates the defined port(s), that is: the defined port(s) are ignored, and only all other logs are analyzed.

timeout.description: The timeout value for the service in seconds

minSource.description: For IP, TCP and UDP Services: the minimum source port number, ICMP Service: the ICMP type number.

maxSource.description: For IP, TCP and UDP Services: the maximum source port number.
